content_markdown: The temperature of an object is $-153^{\circ}\text{C}$. Its temperature is raised to $273^{\circ}\text{C}$. What is the temperature change of the object?
vibe_explanation: A temperature change in Celsius is numerically equal to a temperature change in Kelvin. To find the change, subtract the initial temperature from the final temperature: $273 - (-153) = 426$. Therefore, the change is $426^{\circ}\text{C}$, which is equivalent to $426\text{ K}$.
